speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Now say, Chatillion, what would France with us?

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Thus, after greeting, speaks the King of France

speaker: CHATILLION
text: In my behavior to the majesty,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: The borrowed majesty, of England here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: A strange beginning: “borrowed majesty”!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Silence, good mother. Hear the embassy.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Philip of France, in right and true behalf

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Of thy deceasèd brother Geoffrey’s son,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Arthur Plantagenet, lays most lawful claim

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To this fair island and the territories,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To Ireland, Poitiers, Anjou, Touraine, Maine,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Desiring thee to lay aside the sword

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Which sways usurpingly these several titles,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: And put the same into young Arthur’s hand,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Thy nephew and right royal sovereign.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What follows if we disallow of this?

speaker: CHATILLION
text: The proud control of fierce and bloody war,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: To enforce these rights so forcibly withheld.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Here have we war for war and blood for blood,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Controlment for controlment: so answer France.

speaker: CHATILLION
text: Then take my king’s defiance from my mouth,

speaker: CHATILLION
text: The farthest limit of my embassy.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Bear mine to him, and so depart in peace.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Be thou as lightning in the eyes of France,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For ere thou canst report, I will be there;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The thunder of my cannon shall be heard.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: So, hence. Be thou the trumpet of our wrath

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And sullen presage of your own decay.—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: An honorable conduct let him have.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Pembroke, look to ’t.—Farewell, Chatillion.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: What now, my son! Have I not ever said

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: How that ambitious Constance would not cease

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Till she had kindled France and all the world

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Upon the right and party of her son?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: This might have been prevented and made whole

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: With very easy arguments of love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Which now the manage of two kingdoms must

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: With fearful bloody issue arbitrate.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Our strong possession and our right for us.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Your strong possession much more than your right,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Or else it must go wrong with you and me—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: So much my conscience whispers in your ear,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Which none but God and you and I shall hear.

speaker: ESSEX
text: My liege, here is the strangest controversy

speaker: ESSEX
text: Come from the country to be judged by you

speaker: ESSEX
text: That e’er I heard. Shall I produce the men?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Let them approach.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Our abbeys and our priories shall pay

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: This expedition’s charge.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What men are you?

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Your faithful subject I, a gentleman,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Born in Northamptonshire, and eldest son,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: As I suppose, to Robert Faulconbridge,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: A soldier, by the honor-giving hand

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Of Coeur de Lion knighted in the field.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What art thou?

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: The son and heir to that same Faulconbridge.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Is that the elder, and art thou the heir?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: You came not of one mother then, it seems.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Most certain of one mother, mighty king—

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That is well known—and, as I think, one father.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: But for the certain knowledge of that truth

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: I put you o’er to heaven and to my mother.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Of that I doubt, as all men’s children may.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Out on thee, rude man! Thou dost shame thy mother

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: And wound her honor with this diffidence.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: I, madam? No, I have no reason for it.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That is my brother’s plea, and none of mine,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: The which if he can prove, he pops me out

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: At least from fair five hundred pound a year.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Heaven guard my mother’s honor and my land!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A good blunt fellow.—Why, being younger born,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Doth he lay claim to thine inheritance?

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: I know not why, except to get the land.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: But once he slandered me with bastardy.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: But whe’er I be as true begot or no,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That still I lay upon my mother’s head.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: But that I am as well begot, my liege—

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Fair fall the bones that took the pains for me!—

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Compare our faces and be judge yourself.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: If old Sir Robert did beget us both

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: And were our father, and this son like him,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: O, old Sir Robert, father, on my knee

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: I give heaven thanks I was not like to thee!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Why, what a madcap hath heaven lent us here!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: He hath a trick of Coeur de Lion’s face;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: The accent of his tongue affecteth him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Do you not read some tokens of my son

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: In the large composition of this man?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Mine eye hath well examinèd his parts

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And finds them perfect Richard.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Sirrah, speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What doth move you to claim your brother’s land?

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Because he hath a half-face, like my father.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: With half that face would he have all my land—

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: A half-faced groat five hundred pound a year!

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: My gracious liege, when that my father lived,

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Your brother did employ my father much—

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Well, sir, by this you cannot get my land.

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Your tale must be how he employed my mother.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: And once dispatched him in an embassy

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: To Germany, there with the Emperor

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: To treat of high affairs touching that time.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Th’ advantage of his absence took the King

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: And in the meantime sojourned at my father’s;

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Where how he did prevail I shame to speak.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: But truth is truth: large lengths of seas and shores

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Between my father and my mother lay,

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: As I have heard my father speak himself,

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: When this same lusty gentleman was got.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Upon his deathbed he by will bequeathed

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: His lands to me, and took it on his death

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That this my mother’s son was none of his;

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: An if he were, he came into the world

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Full fourteen weeks before the course of time.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Then, good my liege, let me have what is mine,

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: My father’s land, as was my father’s will.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Sirrah, your brother is legitimate.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Your father’s wife did after wedlock bear him,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: An if she did play false, the fault was hers,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Which fault lies on the hazards of all husbands

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That marry wives. Tell me, how if my brother,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Who as you say took pains to get this son,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Had of your father claimed this son for his?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In sooth, good friend, your father might have kept

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: This calf, bred from his cow, from all the world;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: In sooth he might. Then if he were my brother’s,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: My brother might not claim him, nor your father,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Being none of his, refuse him. This concludes:

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: My mother’s son did get your father’s heir;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Your father’s heir must have your father’s land.

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Shall then my father’s will be of no force

speaker:
ROBERT
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: To dispossess that child which is not his?

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Of no more force to dispossess me, sir,

speaker:
PHILIP
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Than was his will to get me, as I think.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Whether hadst thou rather: be a Faulconbridge

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: And, like thy brother, to enjoy thy land,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Or the reputed son of Coeur de Lion,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Lord of thy presence, and no land besides?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Madam, an if my brother had my shape

speaker: BASTARD
text: And I had his, Sir Robert’s his like him,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And if my legs were two such riding-rods,

speaker: BASTARD
text: My arms such eel-skins stuffed, my face so thin

speaker: BASTARD
text: That in mine ear I durst not stick a rose,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Lest men should say “Look where three-farthings goes,”

speaker: BASTARD
text: And, to his shape, were heir to all this land,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Would I might never stir from off this place,

speaker: BASTARD
text: I would give it every foot to have this face.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I would not be Sir Nob in any case.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: I like thee well. Wilt thou forsake thy fortune,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Bequeath thy land to him, and follow me?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: I am a soldier and now bound to France.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Brother, take you my land. I’ll take my chance.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your face hath got five hundred pound a year,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Yet sell your face for five pence and ’tis dear.—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Madam, I’ll follow you unto the death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Nay, I would have you go before me thither.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Our country manners give our betters way.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What is thy name?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Philip, my liege, so is my name begun,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Philip, good old Sir Robert’s wife’s eldest son.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: From henceforth bear his name whose form thou bearest.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Kneel thou down Philip, but rise more great.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Arise Sir Richard and Plantagenet.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Brother by th’ mother’s side, give me your hand.

speaker: BASTARD
text: My father gave me honor, yours gave land.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now blessèd be the hour, by night or day,

speaker: BASTARD
text: When I was got, Sir Robert was away!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: The very spirit of Plantagenet!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: I am thy grandam, Richard. Call me so.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Madam, by chance but not by truth. What though?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Something about, a little from the right,

speaker: BASTARD
text: In at the window, or else o’er the hatch.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Who dares not stir by day must walk by night,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And have is have, however men do catch.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Near or far off, well won is still well shot,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And I am I, howe’er I was begot.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Go, Faulconbridge, now hast thou thy desire.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A landless knight makes thee a landed squire.—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Come, madam,—and come, Richard. We must speed

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For France, for France, for it is more than need.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Brother, adieu, good fortune come to thee,

speaker: BASTARD
text: For thou wast got i’ th’ way of honesty.

speaker: BASTARD
text: A foot of honor better than I was,

speaker: BASTARD
text: But many a many foot of land the worse.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Well, now can I make any Joan a lady.

speaker: BASTARD
text: “Good den, Sir Richard!” “God-a-mercy, fellow!”

speaker: BASTARD
text: An if his name be George, I’ll call him “Peter,”

speaker: BASTARD
text: For new-made honor doth forget men’s names;

speaker: BASTARD
text: ’Tis too respective and too sociable

speaker: BASTARD
text: For your conversion. Now your traveler,

speaker: BASTARD
text: He and his toothpick at my Worship’s mess,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And when my knightly stomach is sufficed,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Why then I suck my teeth and catechize

speaker: BASTARD
text: My pickèd man of countries: “My dear sir,”

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thus leaning on mine elbow I begin,

speaker: BASTARD
text: “I shall beseech you”—that is Question now,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And then comes Answer like an absey-book:

speaker: BASTARD
text: “O, sir,” says Answer, “at your best command,

speaker: BASTARD
text: At your employment, at your service, sir.”

speaker: BASTARD
text: “No, sir,” says Question, “I, sweet sir, at yours.”

speaker: BASTARD
text: And so, ere Answer knows what Question would,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Saving in dialogue of compliment

speaker: BASTARD
text: And talking of the Alps and Apennines,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The Pyrenean and the river Po,

speaker: BASTARD
text: It draws toward supper in conclusion so.

speaker: BASTARD
text: But this is worshipful society

speaker: BASTARD
text: And fits the mounting spirit like myself;

speaker: BASTARD
text: For he is but a bastard to the time

speaker: BASTARD
text: That doth not smack of observation,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And so am I whether I smack or no;

speaker: BASTARD
text: And not alone in habit and device,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Exterior form, outward accouterment,

speaker: BASTARD
text: But from the inward motion to deliver

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sweet, sweet, sweet poison for the age’s tooth,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Which though I will not practice to deceive,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Yet to avoid deceit I mean to learn,

speaker: BASTARD
text: For it shall strew the footsteps of my rising.

speaker: BASTARD
text: But who comes in such haste in riding robes?

speaker: BASTARD
text: What woman post is this? Hath she no husband

speaker: BASTARD
text: That will take pains to blow a horn before her?

speaker: BASTARD
text: O me, ’tis my mother.—How now, good lady?

speaker: BASTARD
text: What brings you here to court so hastily?

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Where is that slave thy brother? Where is he

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That holds in chase mine honor up and down?

speaker: BASTARD
text: My brother Robert, old Sir Robert’s son?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Colbrand the Giant, that same mighty man?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Is it Sir Robert’s son that you seek so?

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: “Sir Robert’s son”? Ay, thou unreverent boy,

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Sir Robert’s son. Why scorn’st thou at Sir Robert?

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: He is Sir Robert’s son, and so art thou.

speaker: BASTARD
text: James Gurney, wilt thou give us leave awhile?

speaker: GURNEY
text: Good leave, good Philip.

speaker: BASTARD
text: “Philip Sparrow,” James.

speaker: BASTARD
text: There’s toys abroad. Anon I’ll tell thee more.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Madam, I was not old Sir Robert’s son.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sir Robert might have eat his part in me

speaker: BASTARD
text: Upon Good Friday and ne’er broke his fast.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sir Robert could do well—marry, to confess—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Could he get me. Sir Robert could not do it;

speaker: BASTARD
text: We know his handiwork. Therefore, good mother,

speaker: BASTARD
text: To whom am I beholding for these limbs?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Sir Robert never holp to make this leg.

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Hast thou conspirèd with thy brother too,

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: That for thine own gain shouldst defend mine honor?

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: What means this scorn, thou most untoward knave?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Knight, knight, good mother, Basilisco-like.

speaker: BASTARD
text: What, I am dubbed! I have it on my shoulder.

speaker: BASTARD
text: But, mother, I am not Sir Robert’s son.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I have disclaimed Sir Robert and my land.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Legitimation, name, and all is gone.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Then, good my mother, let me know my father—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Some proper man, I hope. Who was it, mother?

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Hast thou denied thyself a Faulconbridge?

speaker: BASTARD
text: As faithfully as I deny the devil.

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: King Richard Coeur de Lion was thy father.

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: By long and vehement suit I was seduced

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: To make room for him in my husband’s bed.

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Heaven lay not my transgression to my charge!

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Thou art the issue of my dear offense,

speaker:
LADY
               
              FAULCONBRIDGE
text: Which was so strongly urged past my defense.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now, by this light, were I to get again,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Madam, I would not wish a better father.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Some sins do bear their privilege on Earth,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And so doth yours. Your fault was not your folly.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Needs must you lay your heart at his dispose,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Subjected tribute to commanding love,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Against whose fury and unmatchèd force

speaker: BASTARD
text: The aweless lion could not wage the fight,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Nor keep his princely heart from Richard’s hand.

speaker: BASTARD
text: He that perforce robs lions of their hearts

speaker: BASTARD
text: May easily win a woman’s. Ay, my mother,

speaker: BASTARD
text: With all my heart I thank thee for my father.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Who lives and dares but say thou didst not well

speaker: BASTARD
text: When I was got, I’ll send his soul to hell.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Come, lady, I will show thee to my kin,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And they shall say when Richard me begot,

speaker: BASTARD
text: If thou hadst said him nay, it had been sin.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Who says it was, he lies. I say ’twas not.
